description_long: This object is a classic revolver-style firearm with a long, octagonal barrel and a dark metallic finish. The frame and barrel are constructed from blackened steel, while the grip is made of polished wood, giving it a traditional and robust appearance. The cylinder is prominent and features multiple chambers, typical of a revolver mechanism. The trigger guard is rounded and metallic, encasing a curved trigger. The hammer is exposed and easily accessible for manual cocking. The overall design is reminiscent of 19th-century Colt or Remington revolvers, with a sleek, elongated barrel and ergonomic wooden grip.
description: A black, steel revolver with a long octagonal barrel and polished wooden grip, featuring a prominent cylinder and rounded trigger guard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow barrel opening and the symmetrical alignment of the barrel and grip.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the octagonal barrel, the wooden grip, the exposed hammer, and the detailed cylinder.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the back of the grip and the hammer, with the cylinder partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other profile of the barrel, grip, and trigger guard, mirroring view 1.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, long octagonal barrel with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_stock_type: full stock (wooden grip extending to the base of the frame)
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized metallic guard with smooth edges and no finger grooves
